title: English as a Second Language - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > English as a Second Language 50 Matching Service Providers ADELANTE MUJERES ADULT EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a holistic education program for Latina women working towards a GED, including coursework in English Language Development (ELD), mathematics, grammar, parenting, and an 18-week seminar focused on conflict resolution (ESPERE). The Early Childhood Education program provides preschool and child care for participants' children. 2030 Main Street Suite A Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Low-income Spanish-speaking women and their children, ages 6 weeks-5 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Classes are held 5 days per week for a full year Email (503) 992-0078 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BEAVERTON LITERACY COUNCIL ENGLISH CLASSES FOR IMMIGRANTS AND REFUGEES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers English as a second language courses in person and online. Eligibility: Age 18 and older who are immigrants or refugees Hours: Classes offered twice-per week September-May Online: Class times vary In person: Class times vary; locations are: -5150 SW Watson Avenue, Beaverton, OR, 97005 -13600 SW Allen Boulevard , Beaverton, OR 97005 -17435 NW West Union Road, Portland, OR 97229 -15050 SW Weir Road, Beaverton, OR 97007 Email (503) 468-7809 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLUE MOUNTAIN COMMUNITY COLLEGE ESL AND EL CIVICS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF English as a Second Language (ESL): Provides English instruction to non-native English speakers. Students are taught speaking, reading, and writing skills. English Literacy through Civics (EL Civics): Program for non-native English speakers that emphasizes contextualized instruction on the rights and responsibilities of citizenship, naturalization procedures, civic participation, and US history and government to help students acquire the skills and knowledge to become active and informed parents, workers, and community members. Locations are in Pendleton, Hermiston, Milton-Freewater, Baker City, Boardman, La Grande, and John Day. 2411 NW Carden Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Must be at least age 16 and not enrolled in school. Students under age 18 must have a release from school district in which they reside. Hours: Program schedule varies Email (541) 276-1260 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGING CULTURES ADULT ESL CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides English as a Second Language classes (ESL) in partnership with Clackamas Community College. Also provides a meal for students and their families. Classes include children's activities, homework help, and reading practice for children of students. Volunteer opportunities are available. 1382 SE 3rd Avenue Canby, OR 97013 Eligibility: Adults 18 and older and youth ages 16-18. Must pre-register. Children's activities and homework help is open to children of students ages 4 and up. English language learners from any language can attend. Hours: Class: Monday/Wednesday 6:15pm-8pm Meal: 5:45pm Email (971) 825-2477 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASA LATINOS UNIDOS COMMUNITY BUILDING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides leadership development, education such as English as a Second Language classes (ESL), financial literacy, and civic engagement. 3460 Midvale Drive Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Members of the Latinx community Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 512-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASA LATINOS UNIDOS LEBANON OFFICE COMMUNITY BUILDING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides leadership development, education such as English as a Second Language classes (ESL), financial literacy, and civic engagement. 500 Weldwood Drive Building E Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Members of the Latinx community Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 512-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. Campuses are located in Bend, Redmond, Madras, and Prineville. 2600 NW College Way Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 383-7700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRO CULTURAL OF WASHINGTON COUNTY ESL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Hosts Goodwill Industries English as a Second Language (ESL) classes. 1110 N Adair Street Cornelius, OR 97113 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Administration: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-1pm and 2pm-6pm, Friday 8:30am-12:30pm Classes: Wednesday-Thursday 11am-1pm Email (503) 359-0446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHEMEKETA COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and ESL classes. Main campuses are located in Salem and McMinnville. 4000 Lancaster Drive NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Class times vary Email (503) 399-5000 Get Directions Visit Website More education and professional development classes, and English as a Second Language classes. Campus locations in Astoria and Seaside. 1651 Lexington Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 338-2411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA GORGE COMMUNITY COLLEGE COLUMBIA GORGE COMMUNITY COLLEGE THE DALLES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. 400 E Scenic Drive Building 2, Room 2.035 The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-6pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 506-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORVALLIS MULTICULTURAL LITERACY CENTER INFORMATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides English language assistance, tutoring, citizenship training and language reference materials, as well as a social environment for people from different cultures to connect and share. 2638 NW Jackson Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday-Friday 10am-3pm Email (541) 632-3890 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERSEALS OREGON EASTERSEALS CONNECTING COMMUNITIES PROGRAM EUGENE CONNECTING COMMUNITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services for individuals with disabilities who have language and/or literacy barriers. Offers workshops for job application completion, mock interviews, resume building, ESL classes, and computer classes, and helps to find and retain employment. 115 W 8th Aveune Suite 330 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: ESL and Computer Literacy Classes: Unrestricted Employment Program: -18 years of age and older -Documented disability -Able to work legally in United States -For individuals who have a language barrier or speak English as a second language Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 636-4027 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERSEALS OREGON EASTERSEALS OREGON PORTLAND CONNECTING COMMUNITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services for individuals with disabilities who have language and/or literacy barriers. Offers workshops for job application completion, mock interviews, resume building, ESL classes, and computer classes, and helps to find and retain employment. 237 NE Broadway Street Suite 100 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: ESL and Computer Literacy Classes: Unrestricted Employment Program: -18 years of age and older -Documented disability -Able to work legally in United States -For individuals who have a language barrier or speak English as a second language Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (502) 222-8500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERSEALS OREGON EASTERSEALS OREGON SALEM CONNECTING COMMUNITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services for individuals with disabilities who have language and/or literacy barriers. Offers workshops for job application completion, mock interviews, resume building, ESL classes, and computer classes, and helps to find and retain employment. 3878 Beverly Avenue NE Building H, Suite 5 Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: ESL and Computer Literacy Classes: Unrestricted Employment Program: -18 years of age and older -Documented disability -Able to work legally in United States -For individuals who have a language barrier or speak English as a second language Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 304-7140 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FRIENDS OF REFUGEES ENGLISH AS A SECOND LANGUAGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides English as Second Language (ESL) classes. Childcare and transportation are available depending on distance and current staffing levels. Eligibility: Age 18 and older refugees and asylum-seekers, all others can inquire about availability on a case by case basis Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 9:30am-11:30am Email (503) 858-4042 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOODWILL JOB CONNECTION ENGLISH AS A SECOND LANGUAGE CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers English as a Second Language (ESL) classes to anyone who wishes to learn or improve their English skills. Also provides assistance to students preparing for their naturalization interview. 1943 SE Sixth Avenue Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by location (503) 238-6100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO EASTERN OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information, resource referral, and wraparound services for refugee families. Programming includes refugee employment services, ESL classes, and youth refugee mentorship. 723 S Oregon Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Immigrants, refugees, and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 222-5391 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. 7390 S 6th Street Klamath Falls, OR 97603 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 882-3521 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transfer degrees, 40+ career technical programs, and a variety of non-credit courses. Academic counseling and Career Information System (CIS), which lists job occupations of interest are offered as well. Provides career planning, academic, and personal advising, adult basic education, various instructional programs, disability services, telecourses, and distance learning. 4000 E 30th Avenue Building 24 Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 463-3000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides workforce development assistance including computer training, tutoring in English or ESL classes, application assistance, resume assistance, small business development workshops and advice, and information and referral for employment and training opportunities. 2680 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 110 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus is on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 10am-5pm, Tuesday 10am-7pm Email (541) 382-4366 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION MADRAS WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides workforce development assistance including computer training, tutoring in English or ESL classes, application assistance, resume assistance, small business development workshops and advice, and information and referral for employment and training opportunities. 231 SE 6th Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus is on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-5pm or by appointment, Wednesday 10am-7pm Email (541) 598-6778 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION PRINEVILLE WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides English tutoring, ESL, and computer classes. Also offers help with resumes, job applications, small business workshops, and related advice. 185 NW 4th Street Suite 1 Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus is on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday-Wednesday 10am-5pm or by appointment, Thursday 10am-7pm Email (541) 788-4829 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION LATINO COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION REDMOND WORKFORCE EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides workforce development assistance including computer training, tutoring in English or ESL classes, application assistance, resume assistance, small business development workshops and advice, and information and referral for employment and training opportunities. 636 NW Cedar Avenue Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but focus is on individuals who identify as Latino or Hispanic Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-5pm or by appointment Email (541) 316-5456 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN BENTON COMMUNITY COLLEGE GED AND ADULT BASIC EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers classes to help native and non-native speakers improve their math, reading, writing, listening, and speaking skills. Classes include Adult Basic Education, GED preparation, Spanish GED preparation, skill improvement, and English as a Second Language. The main campus is in Albany with centers in Corvallis, Lebanon, and Sweet Home. 6500 Pacific Boulevard SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Age 16 and older Hours: Varies by location, visit website or call for information on current term offerings Email (541) 917-4710 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LITERACY COUNCIL FOR JOSEPHINE COUNTY ADULT BASIC EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one tutoring. Students learn to read, write, learn English as a second language, improve basic math skills, and prepare for the citizenship test. Assists to improve job skills, pass the written driver's exam, fill out job applications and other forms, read medicine labels and signs, and learn to use a computer in an educational capacity. Offers a specialized program designed to help those with the learning disability dyslexia learn to read, write, and spell. Also helps students qualify for enrollment at Rogue Community College (RCC) and assists them in preparing for their GED in language arts, math, science, and social studies. 132 NE B Street Suite 23 Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 472-0355 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-VALLEY LITERACY CENTER ADULT LITERACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides literacy services. Offers computer basics, adult English classes for English Language Learners, workplace English and Spanish Language Literacy, preparation for GED, United States citizenship, and Certified Nursing Assistants certifications. 1850 45th Avenue NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-8pm Email (503) 463-1488 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MT HOOD COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and ESL classes. 26000 SE Stark Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Class times vary (503) 491-6422 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. Main campus is in Newport with locations in Lincoln City and Waldport. 400 SE College Way Newport, OR 97366 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 867-8501 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES ADULT EDUCATION CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers adult education programs including: English as a Second Languages classes, Oregon Drivers License exam, Citizenship exam, and computer literacy classes. 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted; primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Varies by service (541) 686-8483 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES YOUTH & FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational classes, including English as a Second Language (ESL) and literacy classes. After school and summer youth enrichment programs with a focus on connecting with community resources, hearing from presenters on topics of interest, engaging in volunteer service activities, and gaining skills important for the future, such as resume building, scholarship writing, job searching, personal financial management, and navigating the health system. Child care is provided for ESL and literacy classes 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies with a primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Classes and youth enrichment: No standard hours Phone: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-1:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9am-noon (541) 686-8483 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE Also offers English language classes. 515 NE Dekalb Avenue Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: After school program and guitar lessons: Elementary, middle, or high school age students English classes: Age 18 and older Hours: After school program, Wednesdays 3pm-5pm Guitar lessons, Wednesdays 4:30pm-5:30pm Adult English Classes, Wednesdays 6pm-7pm (541) 389-8888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON HEAD START LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM MEDFORD LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program that provides adult English as a Second Language (ESL) instruction and adult basic education for parents. Children participate in the program with their parents by attending developmentally appropriate classes and getting homework help and Spanish reading support. 1001 Beall Lane Central Point, OR 97502 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-15 Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 5:30pm-8pm (541) 734-5150 1049 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHWESTERN OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. 1988 Newmark Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 888-2525 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHWESTERN OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE SOUTHWESTERN OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE CURRY CAMPUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. 96082 Lone Ranch Parkway Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 813-1667 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK BAY COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. Campuses are located in Tillamook, Rockaway Beach, and Cloverdale. 4301 Third Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm (503) 842-8222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TORUS TORUS ENGLISH CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides virtual and in person English as a second language (ESL) courses for limited English proficient (LEP) individuals. Offers volunteering opportunities to adults who are interested in teaching English. Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Rockwood Library: Mondays 2pm-3:30pm Holgate Library: Tuesdays 6pm-7:30pm Midland Library: Wednesdays 6pm-7:30pm Gresham Library: Thursdays 2pm-3:30pm Email (503) 753-6129 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TREASURE VALLEY COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. The main campus is located in Ontario, Oregon, with a center in Caldwell, Idaho. 650 College Boulevard Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 881-8822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMPQUA COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Umpqua Community College's Adult Basic Skills Development program helps adults who want to get their GED, improve their reading and math skills, prepare for college or a career, and learn English as a Second Language (ESL). Offers classes for people who have earned their diploma or GED and want to improve their basic education skills. Classes are held in various areas, including Roseburg, Glide, and Tri City/Myrtle Creek. ESL classes are held in Roseburg and Tri City/Myrtle Creek. Free tutoring program, school-to-career program, and several computer labs also availble to students. 1140 Umpqua College Road Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Age 16 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 440-4600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMPQUA VALLEY DISABILITIES NETWORK PROJECT LITERACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Tutoring and adult literacy program that offers one-on-one and small group instruction in reading, writing, GED preparation, and English as a Second Language (ESL) courses. 736 SE Jackson Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Adults with disabilities and adults who identify as having low literacy skills Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 672-6336 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOZ WORKERS' RIGHTS EDUCATION PROJECT VOZ WORKER CENTER AND ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE VOZ WORKERS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a hire site center for day laborers. Minimum wage is $22 per hour for general labor.
